parameters.name: type
parameters.label: Primitive Type
parameters.description: For information on the different types, see the and articles. Depending on the primitive type chosen, some options may not apply. Using the ' ' primitive type is not recommended when using instancing.

parameters.name: orient
parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description: The plane on which the circle lies.

parameters.name: modifybounds
parameters.label: Modify Bounds
parameters.description: Available only when an input is connected to the Curcle to set bounds for the sphere. When Modify Bounds = On the transform parameters below will further modify the radius and position of the bounds.

parameters.name: anchoru
parameters.label: Anchor U
parameters.description: Set the point in X about which the geometry is positioned, scaled and rotated.

parameters.name: anchorv
parameters.label: Anchor V
parameters.description: Set the point in Y about which the geometry is positioned, scaled and rotated.

parameters.name: order
parameters.label: Order
parameters.description: If a spline curve is selected, it is built at this order.

parameters.name: divs
parameters.label: Divisions
parameters.description: The number of edges (points +1) used to describe the circle. This option applies to polygons and imperfect splines. The more a circle has, the smoother it looks. Using three divisions makes a triangle

parameters.name: arc
parameters.label: Arc Type
parameters.description: Determines how the circle should be drawn. Applies to polygons and imperfect splines only.

parameters.name: imperfect
parameters.label: Imperfect
parameters.description: This option applies only to Bezier and NURBS circles. If selected, the circles are approximated non-rational curves, otherwise they are perfect rational closed curves.

parameters.name: rady
parameters.label: Rady
parameters.description: The Radius of the Circle in the X and Y directions.

parameters.name: endangle
parameters.label: Endangle
parameters.description: The beginning and ending angles of the arc. An arc will start at the beginning angle, and proceed towards the ending angle. If beginning=0 and end=360 it will be a full circle. As a reference:  Image:

parameters.name: tz
parameters.label: Tz
parameters.description: The Center of the Circle in X, Y and Z.

examples.label: Create a half-arc
examples.description: Create an open arc spanning 180 degrees.

examples.label: Animate circle radius
examples.description: Use as expression on radius to pulse between 0.2 and 0.5.
